title: cenocline cinerea
intro: Cenocline cinerea, also known as the Ashy Cenocline, is a perennial plant species in the borage family that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and roots, which exhibit antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial activities. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and alleviate digestive problems. In traditional African herbal medicine, Cenocline cinerea is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
